bull.t: Clinical Development Progress And Market Potential
bull.b: A planned 120-patient Phase 2b/3 multiple-dose trial, combined with no currently approved therapies for this condition and a differentiated mechanism, creates a clear clinical catalyst and sizable commercial opportunity if efficacy is confirmed.
Phase 2 results demonstrated meaningful pain reduction with urcosimod while maintaining a clean safety profile, supporting potential clinical benefit for patients with neuropathic corneal pain.

FDA endorsement of the pain scale as a clinically meaningful endpoint, together with agreement on study design, sample size and statistical guidance, increases the chance of a robust pivotal trial and clearer regulatory pathway.

bear.t: Limited Data On Corneal Nerve Findings
bear.b: Corneal image improvements are derived from a small exploratory analysis and require confirmation in larger trials, leaving the nerve restoration signal uncertain.
The program faces risks across clinical, regulatory, commercial, financing and intellectual property areas, any of which could delay development or limit future market access.
